Thinking about which balloon to burst first makes subproblems dependent (neighbors change). What if you think about which to burst LAST instead?
Pad the array with 1s at both ends: [1, ...nums, 1]. Define dp[left][right] for the open interval (left, right).
If balloon k is the last to burst in (left, right), coins = arr[left]*arr[k]*arr[right] + dp[left][k] + dp[k][right].
Iterate by interval length from 1 to n. For each interval, try every k as the last balloon. Time: O(n^3).
